project.name: GitLab Fronting Showcase 0.2.0
project.summary: Canonical GitLab fronting showcase package for governed project context, merge request review preparation, issue preparation, approval boundaries, audit evidence, and safe denial of raw GitLab tool access.

risk_notes: Native API backend supply for the governed capability.
MCP alternate backend supply for the governed capability.

data.approval_posture_summary: Write-adjacent actions stop at preview or approval before downstream mutation.
business_goals: Keep raw backend operations behind adapter seams.
Require clarification, audit, and approval where needed.
data.business_problem: Raw gitlab tools expose broad backend power and force teams to encode safety behavior in prompts or skills.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The service accepts only reviewed capability calls, validates required inputs, denies unsupported effects, and records audit lineage.
multi_step_composition_rules: Prefer native API adapters by default.
Use MCP adapters only as an alternate backend implementation seam.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed ANIP fronting layer for gitlab so agents use reviewed capabilities instead of raw API or MCP tools.
data.success_outcome_summary: Generated packages verify with zero blockers and agents see only governed ANIP capabilities.
data.why_now: MCP and native APIs expose useful integration supply, but the organization needs a centralized governed behavior layer.

actors.action_expectations: Can request governed reads and prepare/request flows; cannot bypass approval or call raw backend tools directly.
actors.approval_expectations: Write-adjacent operations require preview or approval before mutation.
actors.notes: Seeded by the fronting showcase batch from reviewed capability mappings.
actors.summary: Uses governed gitlab capabilities through ANIP instead of raw backend tools.
actors.title: Fronting Service Consumer
actors.visibility_expectations: Can see bounded results, previews, approvals, and denial reasons within actor-visible scope.

entries.description: Governed gitlab access through curated ANIP capabilities.
entries.label: gitlab governed workspace

data.policy_summary: Allow gitlab_fronting_consumer to use bounded gitlab fronting capabilities; deny raw export, backend bypass, and unapproved mutation.

rules.governed_outcome: Actor may invoke curated gitlab ANIP capabilities with required input validation, audit, and approval boundaries.
rules.notes: No raw API or MCP tool access is granted by Product Design.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: gitlab_fronting_consumer bounded access to gitlab governed workspace should return bounded_result. Actor may invoke curated gitlab ANIP capabilities with required input validation, audit, and approval boundaries. No raw API or MCP tool access is granted by Product Design.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Actor may invoke curated gitlab ANIP capabilities with required input validation, audit, and approval boundaries.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read operations are bounded and audited. Write-adjacent operations stop at preview or require ANIP approval grants before downstream mutation.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: Curated ANIP capabilities sit in front of selected raw API/MCP operations; agents do not receive direct unbounded backend tool access.

data.rationale: Studio batch generated this governed integration-fronting project from reviewed source docs and curated backend mappings.

orchestration_steps.outcome_notes: Classify the user request into a reviewed governed capability.
Clarify missing required inputs before any downstream call.
Use the native API adapter by default or MCP adapter when explicitly configured.
Stop at preview or approval for write-adjacent actions.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: gitlab_fronting_consumer bounded access to gitlab governed workspace should return bounded_result. Actor may invoke curated gitlab ANIP capabilities with required input validation, audit, and approval boundaries. No raw API or MCP tool access is granted by Product Design.
permission_intent_bindings.governed_outcome: Actor may invoke curated gitlab ANIP capabilities with required input validation, audit, and approval boundaries.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read operations are bounded and audited. Write-adjacent operations stop at preview or require ANIP approval grants before downstream mutation.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: Curated ANIP capabilities sit in front of selected raw API/MCP operations; agents do not receive direct unbounded backend tool access.

data.rationale: Studio batch generated this governed integration-fronting project from reviewed source docs and curated backend mappings.

orchestration_steps.outcome_notes: Classify the user request into a reviewed governed capability.
Clarify missing required inputs before any downstream call.
Use the native API adapter by default or MCP adapter when explicitly configured.
Stop at preview or approval for write-adjacent actions.

gitlab-fronting-studio-20260516220707-sc.note: Fronting showcase review classified this as consuming-app orchestration or presentation guidance. The ANIP contract remains the governed service boundary; the app profile owns request framing, routing, or multi-step coordination.

findings.detail: This scenario has more than one step. The contract should state whether one service owns the full flow, or the consuming app coordinates the steps.
findings.recommendation: Choose one reviewed path: make it a service-owned flow, or mark the remaining coordination as app-owned glue.
findings.title: Who owns the multi-step user request?

scenario.name: gitlab_governed_fronting_path
scenario.narrative: A consuming agent uses curated ANIP capabilities for gitlab instead of direct raw API/MCP tools.
orchestration_steps: Classify the user request into a reviewed governed capability.
Clarify missing required inputs before any downstream call.
Use the native API adapter by default or MCP adapter when explicitly configured.
Stop at preview or approval for write-adjacent actions.
